evidence_excerpt: Diagnosis of a type-2 submucosal posterior laryngeal cleft was made on CT; treatment of dysphonia and exertional stridor is expectant, and symptoms were described as relatively benign. No post-diagnosis improvement or deterioration is documented.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Life-long hoarse, breathy voice with exertional stridor; symptoms characterized as relatively benign and managed expectantly, indicating ongoing but limited-impact symptoms.

notes_on_uncertainty: No follow-up after the CT-based diagnosis is provided. Long-term outcome and quality-of-life categories are conservatively inferred from the expectant management plan and benign symptom characterization at diagnosis, but actual post-diagnosis changes are not documented.
